detaileddescription.para: Include planes whose normal is perpendicular to gravity.
Include planes whose normal is parallel to gravity.
Include planes with arbitrary normals.
Include all plane orientations.

briefdescription.para: For non-horizontal planes, setting this flag will result in the top of the plane rectangle being perpendicular to gravity.
If this flag is set, inner planes will be returned; if it is not set, outer planes will be returned.

detaileddescription.para: Include planes semantically tagged as ceiling.
Include planes semantically tagged as floor.
Include planes semantically tagged as wall.
Include all planes that are semantically tagged.
When this flag is set:

listitem.para: MLPlanesQueryGetResultsWithBoundaries returns polygons along with other applicable rectangular planes. MLPlanesReleaseBoundariesList MUST be called before the next call to MLPlanesQueryGetResultsWithBoundaries, otherwise UnspecifiedFailure will be returned.

detaileddescription.para: When this flag is not set:

listitem.para: MLPlanesQueryGetResultsWithBoundaries returns just rectangular planes. No need to call MLPlanesReleaseBoundariesList.

detaileddescription.para: Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Control flags for plane queries.
Control flags for plane queries.

briefdescription.para: Coplanar connected line segments representing the outer boundary of a polygon, an n-sided polygon where n is the number of vertices.

detaileddescription.para: Releases the polygons data owned by the MLPlaneBoundariesList. Also, check MLPlanesQueryFlag_Polygons description for this API's further behavior.

Gets the result of a plane query with boundaries on each plane. After this function has returned successfully, the handle is invalid and should be discarded. Also check MLPlanesQueryFlag_Polygons description for this API's further behavior.
Initiates a plane query.
Destroys a planes tracker.
Creates a planes tracker.
Type used to represent a plane query.
Initializes an MLPlaneBoundariesList structure.
Type to represent polygons of all returned planes.
Type to represent multiple regions on a 2D plane.
Type used to represent a region boundary on a 2D plane.
A plane with width and height.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a MLPlaneBoundariesList structure which is initialize with default values.
A pointer to an MLHandle which will contain the handle to the planes tracker. If this operation fails, out_handle will be ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
Failed to create planes tracker due to an invalid input parameter.
Successfully created planes tracker.
Perception System has not been started.
The application lacks permission.
Failed to create planes tracker due to an unknown reason.
MLHandle to planes tracker to destroy.
Successfully destroyed planes tracker.
Perception System has not been started.

detaileddescription.para:
One of the following flags must be set for the query to be considered valid:
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Vertical flag.
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Horizontal flag.
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Arbitrary flag.
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Semantic_Ceiling flag.
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Semantic_Floor flag.
MLPlanesQueryFlag_Semantic_Wall flag.

parameterdescription.para: Handle produced by MLPlanesCreate().
Pointer to MLPlanesQuery structure containing query parameters.
A pointer to an MLHandle which will contain the handle to the query. If this operation fails, out_handle will be ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
Failed to initiate plane query due to an invalid input parameter.
Successfully initiated plane query.
Perception System has not been started.
Handle produced by MLPlanesCreate().
Handle produced by MLPlanesQueryBegin().
An array of MLPlane structures.
The count of results pointed to by out_results.
A pointer to MLPlaneBoundariesList for the returned polygons. If out_boundaries is NULL, the function call will not return any polygons, otherwise *out_boundaries must be zero initialized.
Successfully completed query.
Query completion is pending. This is not a failure.
Perception System has not been started.
Query timed out due to unknown reason. Make a new query.
Failed to complete query due to an unknown error.
Handle produced by MLPlanesCreate().
Polygons pointer to the #MLPlaneBoundariessList.
Failed to release memory due to an invalid input parameter.
Successfully released memory.
Perception System has not been started.

inbodydescription.para: Plane center.
Plane rotation.
Plane width.
Plane height.
Flags which describe this plane.

Plane ID. All inner planes within an outer plane will have the same ID (outer plane's ID). These IDs are persistent across plane queries unless a map merge occurs. On a map merge, IDs could be different.
Vertices of all line segments.
Number of vertices.
The polygon that defines the region, the boundary vertices in MLPolygon will be in CCW order.
A polygon may contains multiple holes, the boundary vertices in MLPolygon will be in CW order.
Count of the holes.
Plane ID, the same value associating to the ID in MLPlane if they belong to the same plane.
The boundaries in a plane.
Count of boundaries. A plane may contain multiple boundaries each of which defines a region.
List of MLPlaneBoundaries.

API Level:\n 2
Count of MLPlaneBoundaries in the array.

API Level:\n 2
The flags to apply to this query.
The center of the bounding box which defines where planes extraction should occur.
The rotation of the bounding box where planes extraction will occur.

The size of the bounding box where planes extraction will occur. Only planes within 10 meters of the device will be extracted. The maximum value is 20 meters in each direction. A value of (0, 0, 0) will be treated as (20, 20, 20).

The maximum number of results that should be returned. This is also the minimum expected size of the array of results passed to the MLPlanesGetResult function. In the case where the number of rectangular planes exceed this value, only the largest max_results planes will be returned. This can cause the number of polygons returned to be smaller than max_results, as there can be multiple inner planes per planar surface but only one polygon, and if the rectangular plane of a planar surface is not being returned because max_results have been reached, the corresponding polygon won't be returned.
The minimum area (in squared meters) of planes to be returned. This value cannot be lower than 0.04 (lower values will be capped to this minimum). A good default value is 0.25.
